FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// messages are consistently formatted.\n157\t//\n158\t// Parameters:\n159\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n160\t//   - message: The core error message\n161\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n162\t//\n163\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n164\t//\n165\t// Example usage:\n166\t//\n167\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n169\t//\n170\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n180\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n181\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\t// Add field name if present\n185\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n203\t//\n204\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n205\t//\n206\t// Example usage:\n207\t//\n208\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n343\t//\n344\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n345\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n346\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n347\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n348\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// =============================================================================\n352\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n353\t// =============================================================================\n354\t\n355\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n356\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n357\t//\n358\t// Parameters:\n359\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n360\t//\n361\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n362\t//\n363\t// Example usage:\n364\t//\n365\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n366\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n370\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n371\t\tswitch category {\n372\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n395\t//\n396\t// Parameters:\n397\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n398\t//\n399\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n400\t//\n401\t// Example usage:\n402\t//\n403\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n404\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n453\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n462\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n463\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n464\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n465\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n466\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n467\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n468\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n481\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n482\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n486\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n487\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n488\t//\n489\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n490\t//\n491\t// Example usage:\n492\t//\n493\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n494\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n495\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n496\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n497\t//\t}\n498\t//\n499\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n500\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n501\t//\n502\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n503\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n504\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n505\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n506\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n507\t\n508\t\t// Add severity if requested\n509\t\tif includeSeverity {\n510\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n511\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n512\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n513\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n514\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n515\t\t\t} else {\n516\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n517\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n518\t\t\t}\n519\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n520\t\t}\n521\t\n522\t\t// Add error type and field\n523\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n524\t\n525\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n526\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n527\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n528\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n529\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n530\t\t\t}\n531\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n532\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n533\t\t\t}\n534\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n535\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n536\t\t\t}\n537\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n541\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n542\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n543\t\t}\n544\t\n545\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n546\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n547\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n548\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n549\t\n550\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n551\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n552\t\t\t}\n553\t\n554\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n555\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n556\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n557\t\t\t}\n558\t\n559\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n560\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n561\t\t}\n562\t\n563\t\treturn builder.String()\n564\t}\n565\t\n566\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n567\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n568\t//\n569\t// Parameters:\n570\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n571\t//\n572\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n573\t//\n574\t// Example usage:\n575\t//\n576\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n577\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n578\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n579\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n580\t//\t}\n581\t//\n582\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n583\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n584\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n585\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n586\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n587\t\t}\n588\t\n589\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n590\t}\n591\t\n592\t// =============================================================================\n593\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n594\t// =============================================================================\n595\t\n596\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n597\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n598\t//\n599\t// Parameters:\n600\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n601\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n602\t//\n603\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n604\t//\n605\t// Example usage:\n606\t//\n607\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n608\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n609\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n610\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n611\t//\t}, {\n612\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n613\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n614\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n615\t//\t}}\n616\t//\n617\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n618\t//\t// Returns:\n619\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n620\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n621\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n622\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n623\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n624\t\t}\n625\t\n626\t\tvar lines []string\n627\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n628\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n629\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n630\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n631\t\t}\n632\t\n633\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n634\t}\n635\t\n636\t// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n637\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n638\t//\n639\t// Parameters:\n640\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n641\t//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n642\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n643\t//\n644\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n645\t//\n646\t// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n647\t//\n648\t// Example usage:\n649\t//\n650\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n651\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n652\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n653\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n654\t//\t}, {\n655\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n656\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n657\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n658\t//\t}}\n659\t//\n660\t//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n661\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n662\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n663\t//\t}\n664\t//\n665\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n666\t//\t// Returns:\n667\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n668\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\n669\tfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n670\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n671\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n672\t\t}\n673\t\n674\t\tvar lines []string\n675\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n676\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n677\t\n678\t\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n679\t\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n680\t\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n681\t\t\t}\n682\t\n683\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n684\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n685\t\t}\n686\t\n687\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n688\t}\n689\t\n690\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n691\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n692\t//\n693\t// Parameters:\n694\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n695\t//\n696\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n697\t//\n698\t// Example usage:\n699\t//\n700\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n701\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n702\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n703\t//\t}, {\n704\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n705\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n706\t//\t}}\n707\t//\n708\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n709\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n710\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n711\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n712\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n713\t\t}\n714\t\n715\t\t// Count errors by type\n716\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n717\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n718\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n719\t\t}\n720\t\n721\t\t// Build summary parts\n722\t\tvar parts []string\n723\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n724\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n725\t\t}\n726\t\n727\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n728\t}\n729\t\n730\t// =============================================================================\n731\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n732\t// =============================================================================\n733\t\n734\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n735\t// without the parent parameter.\n736\t//\n737\t// Parameters:\n738\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n739\t//   - location: Additional location context\n740\t//\n741\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n742\t//\n743\t// Example usage:\n744\t//\n745\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n746\t//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\n747\tfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n748\t\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n752\t// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n753\t//\n754\t// Parameters:\n755\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n756\t//\n757\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n758\t//\n759\t// Example usage:\n760\t//\n761\t//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n762\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n763\tfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n764\t\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n768\t// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n769\t//\n770\t// Parameters:\n771\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n772\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n773\t//\n774\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n775\tfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n776\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n777\t}\n778\t\n779\t// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n780\t// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n781\t// of formatting an error with context.\n782\t//\n783\t// Parameters:\n784\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n785\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n786\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n787\t//\n788\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n789\t//\n790\t// Example usage:\n791\t//\n792\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n793\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err, true, ctx)\n794\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n795\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n796\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n797\t}\n798\t\n799\t// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n800\t//\n801\t// Parameters:\n802\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n803\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n804\t//\n805\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n806\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n807\t\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n808\t}\n809\t\n810\t// =============================================================================\n811\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n812\t// =============================================================================\n813\t\n814\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n815\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n816\t//\n817\t// Parameters:\n818\t//   - value: The value to quote\n819\t//\n820\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n821\t//\n822\t// Example usage:\n823\t//\n824\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n825\t//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n826\t//\n827\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n828\t//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\n829\tfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n830\t\tif value == \"\" {\n831\t\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n832\t\t}\n833\t\n834\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n835\t}\n836\t\n837\t// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n838\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n839\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n840\t//\n841\t// Parameters:\n842\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n843\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n844\t//\n845\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n846\t//\n847\t// Example usage:\n848\t//\n849\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n850\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n851\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n852\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n853\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n854\t\t}\n855\t\n856\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n857\t\t\treturn value\n858\t\t}\n859\t\n860\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n861\t}\n862\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c20395a2-d11a-436b-9966-fbff614d6111","uuid":"dd873d4e-e501-44a6-bec7-20efb5ae7437","timestamp":"2026-07-15T19:41:13.875Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_formatting.go","content":"package validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"💡\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"❓\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tvar contextParts []string\n\n\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\nfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\n\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n// of formatting an error with context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err, true, ctx)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n","numLines":862,"startLine":1,"totalLines":862}}}

=============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorList(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname            string\n\t\terrors          []ValidationError\n\t\tincludeSeverity bool\n\t\tcontains        []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"empty list\",\n\t\t\terrors:          []ValidationError{},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: true,\n\t\t\tcontains:        []string{\"No errors\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"multiple errors with severity\",\n\t\t\terrors: []ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\t\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Invalid format\",\n\t\t\t\t\tFieldName: \"password\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: true,\n\t\t\tcontains: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"1.\",\n\t\t\t\t\"2.\",\n\t\t\t\t\"[required] []ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\t\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: false,\n\t\t\tcontains: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"1.\",\n\t\t\t\t\"[required] email\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorList(tt.errors, tt.includeSeverity)\n\t\t\tfor _, expected := range tt.contains {\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, expected) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorList() expected to contain %v, got %v\", expected, result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorListSummary(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\terrors   []ValidationError\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"empty list\",\n\t\t\terrors:   []ValidationError{},\n\t\t\texpected: \"No errors\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"multiple errors tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorListSummary(tt.errors)\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, tt.expected) && tt.expected != \"No errors\" {\n\t\t\t\t// For specific checks, verify key parts\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"error(s)\") {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorListSummary() expected to contain 'error(s)', got %v\", result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif tt.expected == \"No errors\" && result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorListSummary() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTION TESTS\n// TestTruncateString(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\tvalue     string\n\t\tmaxLength int\n\t\texpected  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"short 5,\n\t\t\texpected:  \"exact\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"needs truncation\",\n\t\t\tvalue:     \"very long string here\",\n\t\t\tmaxLength: 10,\n\t\t\texpected:  \"very lo...\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"minimum length\",\n\t\t\tvalue:     \"abcdefghijk\",\n\t\t\tmaxLength: 3,\n\t\t\texpected:  \"...\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := TruncateString(tt.value, tt.maxLength)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"TruncateString() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// INTEGRATION TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormattingIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that formatting functions work well together\n\terr := ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\tExpected:  \"value\",\n\t\tActual:    nil,\n\t\tLocation:  \"line 5\",\n\t}\n\n\t// Format with severity\n\tfullMsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"High\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected severity in formatted error\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"required\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error type in formatted error\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"email\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected field name in formatted error\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Format brief\n\tbriefMsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n\tif !strings.Contains(briefMsg, \"email\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected field name in brief error\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(briefMsg, \"required\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error type in brief error\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FORMATERROR FUNCTION TESTS (from format_helper.go)\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatError(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"format error without field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: error type uses default\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] Something went wrong\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty message uses fallback with field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"validation\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[validation] email: email validation failed\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty message uses fallback without field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] (no message provided)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"status_code error\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"status_code\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"response\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[status_code] response: Expected 200 but got 404\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"unknown error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"custom_type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Custom error message\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[custom_type] field: Custom error message\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tgot := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\t\t\tif got != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorBackwardCompatibility(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that string-based FormatError produces consistent results\n\t// with ErrorType-based FormatErrorWithType\n\tt.Run(\"string and ErrorType functions produce consistent results\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Field is required\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n\n\t\t// String-based function\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"required\", message, fieldName)\n\n\t\t// ErrorType-based function\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeRequired, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String and ErrorType functions should produce same result:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"format error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Invalid format\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"format\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeFormat, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"range error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Value out of range\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"age\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"range\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeRange, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorConsistentClassification(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that all ErrorType enum values are properly classified\n\tt.Run(\"all ErrorType values produce valid output\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tallTypes := []ErrorType{\n\t\t\tErrTypeRequired,\n\t\t\tErrTypeFormat,\n\t\t\tErrTypeRange,\n\t\t\tErrTypeLength,\n\t\t\tErrTypeType,\n\t\t\tErrTypeValue,\n\t\t\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\t\tErrTypeConflict,\n\t\t\tErrTypeUnknown,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, et := range allTypes {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithType(et, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains error type\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, et.String()) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing %v, got %v\", et, et.String(), result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains field name\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"field\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing field name, got %v\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains message\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"Test message\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing message, got %v\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"string-based error types are properly handled\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\terrorTypes := []string{\"required\", \"format\", \"range\", \"type\", \"unknown\"}\n\n\t\tfor _, etStr := range errorTypes {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(etStr, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains error type\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, etStr) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %v should produce output containing error type, got %v\", etStr, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidationErrorContext provides positional and relational context for validation errors.\n// This struct helps identify where in a document or response the validation error occurred\n// and which other fields might be related to the issue.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tctx := ValidationErrorContext{\n//\t    Location: \"field 'user.email' in line 42\",\n//\t    RelatedFields: []string{\"email_confirmation\", \"user.email_format\"},\n//\t}\n//\n//\tif ctx.HasLocation() {\n//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n\t// This field is optional but recommended for complex validation scenarios.\n\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n\t// This field is optional and provides additional debugging context.\n\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// HasLocation checks if a location has been specified in the context.\n// Returns true if Location is non-empty, false otherwise.\nfunc (vec ValidationErrorContext) HasLocation() bool {\n\treturn vec.Location != \"\"\n}\n\n// HasRelatedFields checks if related fields have been specified in the context.\n// Returns true if RelatedFields is non-empty, false otherwise.\nfunc (vec ValidationErrorContext) HasRelatedFields() bool {\n\treturn len(vec.RelatedFields) > 0\n}\n\n// IsEmpty checks if the ValidationErrorContext has no information set.\n// Returns true if both Location is empty and RelatedFields is empty/nil.\nfunc (vec ValidationErrorContext) IsEmpty() bool {\n\treturn !vec.HasLocation() && !vec.HasRelatedFields()\n}\n\n// String returns a string representation of the validation error context.\n// This is useful for debugging and logging validation context information.\nfunc (vec ValidationErrorContext) String() string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\n\tif vec.HasLocation() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", vec.Location))\n\t}\n\n\tif vec.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related_fields: [%s]\", strings.Join(vec.RelatedFields, \", \")))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn Actual: 404,\n//\t}\n//\tif ea.Mismatched() {\n//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Expected %d but got %d\", ea.Expected, ea.Actual)\n//\t}\ntype ExpectedActual struct {\n\t// Expected contains the value that was validation.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// HasExpected checks if an expected value has been set.\n// Returns true if Expected is non-nil, false otherwise.\nfunc (ea ExpectedActual) HasExpected() bool {\n\treturn ea.Expected != nil\n}\n\n// HasActual checks if an actual value has been set.\n// Returns true if Actual Expected and Actual are set and they are not equal.\n// Note: This uses simple equality comparison and may not work correctly for all types.\nfunc (ea ExpectedActual) Mismatched() bool {\n\tif !ea.HasExpected() || !ea.HasActual() {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle specific types appropriately\n\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n\tcase int:\n\t\tif act, ok := ea.Actual.(int); ok {\n\t\t\treturn exp != act\n\t\t}\n\tcase string:\n\t\tif act, ok := ea.Actual.(string); ok {\n\t\t\treturn exp != act\n\t\t}\n\tcase []int:\n\t\tif act, ok := ea.Actual.(int); ok {\n\t\t\t// Check if actual is in the expected list\n\t\t\tfor _, e := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tif e == act {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Default: use simple comparison\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", ea.Expected) != fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", ea.Actual)\n}\n\n// IsEmpty checks if both Expected and Actual values are unset.\n// Returns true if both Expected and Actual are nil, false otherwise.\nfunc (ea ExpectedActual) IsEmpty() bool {\n\treturn !ea.HasExpected() && !ea.HasActual()\n}\n\n// String returns a string representation of the expected vs actual comparison.\n// This formats the comparison in a human-readable way for debugging and logging.\nfunc (ea ExpectedActual) String() string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\n\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\texpectedStr := \"expected: one of [\"\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \", \"\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\texpectedStr += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr += \"]\"\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, expectedStr)\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", exp))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", exp))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif ea.HasActual() {\n\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tif len(act) > 50 {\n\t\t\t\tact = act[:50] + \"...\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", act))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", act))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"ExpectedActual(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"ExpectedActual(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// Validate checks if the ExpectedActual struct is valid.\n// An ExpectedActual is considered valid if at least one of Expected or Actual is set.\n// Returns an error if both fields are nil, nil otherwise.\nfunc (ea ExpectedActual) Validate() error {\n\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n\t\treturn
